cityDNA.coreFantasy: A dangerous reef-harbor civilization where sharkfolk transformed predation into protection, logistics, sacred water, public works, harbor ceremony, and disciplined tide law.
cityDNA.readerTakeaway: Bloodreef is not a pirate port or savage predator city; it is a full maritime capital where dangerous forms guard nurseries, cargo, shrines, harbors, and civic trust.
cityDNA.civilizationThesis: Bloodreef turns predation, nursery protection, tide-sealed government, reef logistics, public works, sacred shrines, and harbor ceremony into a civic order that is both threatening and protective.
cityDNA.visualIdentity: Dark reef stone, black basalt, sharkjaw towers, shell spiral windows, blue lamps, red-and-black banners, protected nursery halls, cargo courts, survey halls, votive lanterns, harbor basilicas, reef canals, and wet stone.
cityDNA.culturalIdentity: A sharkfolk maritime civilization that treats danger as a public responsibility: teeth and towers are not lawlessness, but warnings, protections, and civic marks.
cityDNA.symbolicIdentity: Shark jaws, red reef, tide seals, shell spirals, nursery basins, cargo seals, blue lamps, votive lanterns, black towers, harbor arches, and nursery pillars symbolize predatory force redirected into protection.

canonLocks: Bloodreef is an ancient sharkfolk reef-harbor capital, not a pirate port.
The Wonder is Sharkjaw Tide Citadel of the Red Reef.
The Seat of Power is Shell Spiral Windows Tide-Seal Citadel.
The Beacon is Nursery Hall Beacon.
The five primary districts are Nursery Hall, Warehousing / Logistics, Public Works Authority, Shrine / Sacred Place, and Harbor Campus.

The uploaded district anchors include S1 Nursery Hall, S1 Nursery Pillar, S1 Nursery Kiosk, Warehousing Logistics Gold-Seal Monument, Blue-Lamp Monument, Shrine Sacred Place Lantern Monument, Votive Grove Nook, S5 Harbor Arch, and S5 Harbor Bridgelet.
provisionalCanon: Bloodreef's highest civic virtue is protected ferocity.
The central tension is predatory identity versus nursery protection and public trust.
Nursery space is the city's emotional reversal: the most dangerous silhouettes are built around protecting the most vulnerable civic space.
rejectedReads: Bloodreef is savage, lawless, or only violent.
Bloodreef is merely a pirate city.
The shark imagery is decorative only.
Nursery landmarks can be generalized into ordinary harbor decoration.
cautionFlags: Keep nursery protection central; it is the city’s emotional reversal of predator assumptions.
Use dark predatory architecture without making the city crude.
Keep logistics, public works, shrines, and harbor ceremony visible.
Preserve exact nursery and harbor sidecar anchors rather than replacing them with generic shell markers or approaches.

environment.environmentalLogic: The reef is engineered into a living harbor machine. Water is route, danger, nursery boundary, sacred medium, cargo engine, and defensive edge. The city makes predatory geography legible through controlled thresholds and protected basins.

skyline: Sharkjaw Tide Citadel of the Red Reef as far wonder anchor
Shell Spiral Windows Tide-Seal Citadel as central seat of power
Nursery Hall Beacon as near/front signal landmark
Harbor Basilica as ritual harbor anchor
dark towers, shell windows, reef arches, cargo halls, shrine spires, and canal bridges over turquoise water

civilization.people: Citizens are sharkfolk: direct, water-literate, physically intimidating, protective of young, and accustomed to reading danger as part of civic responsibility.
civilization.governance: Authority is tide-sealed, protective, and maritime. The city governs through Citadel records, nursery law, cargo control, public works permits, shrine legitimacy, and harbor rites.
civilization.religion: Religious expression is water-facing and practical: roadside shrines, votive groves, harbor basilicas, tide memory, bloodline protection, and reef rites all overlap civic duty.
customs: nursery spaces are treated as civic taboo zones
public danger is acknowledged rather than hidden
cargo and tide seals carry legal weight
harbor rites bind arrival and departure
sharkjaw imagery is used as warning and promise rather than threat alone
bodyLanguage: guards reading nursery thresholds before speaking
speechPatterns: ritual phrases around bloodline and reef

streets.appearance: Dark, wet, reef-built streets with black towers, shell spiral windows, blue lamps, nursery thresholds, cargo yards, permit counters, shrine lanterns, basilica stairs, and sharkjaw silhouettes.
streets.movement: Movement follows reef canals, bridgelets, dock roads, nursery approaches, tide gates, survey plazas, shrine paths, harbor arches, and harbor basilica terraces.
streets.lighting: Daylight reflects off water and shell; night is blue-lamped and shrine-lit, with black towers cutting hard silhouettes.
streets.weatherEffects: Storms make the city feel more dangerous and more protective; tide shifts change routes, cargo timing, shrine attendance, and nursery access.
streets.dayCycle: Morning cargo and public works activity; midday permit and nursery traffic; evening shrine lanterns and harbor basilica rituals.
streets.nightCycle: Blue lamps, beacon glow, wet black stone, tide gates, watch towers, nursery thresholds, and votive lights define movement.

housing.wealthy: Citadel-adjacent shell-window residences, black stone terraces, guarded water access, blue lamps, and red reef detailing.
housing.middle: Harbor apartments, reef-canal homes, shell inlay, shared bridgelets, practical water entries, and family courtyards.
housing.poor: Dockside rooms, cargo-adjacent flats, tight wet lanes, shared basins, patched sailcloth shade, and work-oriented reef housing.

districts.name: Nursery Hall
districts.civicPurpose: Protection of young, emotional legitimacy, public warning, and the civic transformation of predator identity into guardianship.
districts.visualIdentity: S1 Nursery Hall, Nursery Hall Beacon, S1 Nursery Pillar, S1 Nursery Kiosk, shell inlay, blue lamps, protected basins, sharkfolk guardians, and controlled thresholds.
districts.emotionalIdentity: Protective, tense, sacred-practical, and unexpectedly tender.

districts.name: Warehousing / Logistics
districts.civicPurpose: Cargo control, inventory, maritime bureaucracy, and public trust in goods.
districts.visualIdentity: Warehousing / Logistics Civic Hall, Cargo Yard, Warehousing Logistics Gold-Seal Monument, Inventory Hall Nook, crates, ropes, docks, and seal counters.

districts.name: Public Works Authority
districts.civicPurpose: Reef engineering, harbor permits, dock repair, tide-gate maintenance, and civic safety.
districts.visualIdentity: Public Works Authority Hall, Survey Hall, Blue-Lamp Monument, Permit Yard Nook, maps, waterline markers, tide-gate diagrams, and wet stone plazas.

districts.name: Shrine / Sacred Place
districts.civicPurpose: Water-facing memory, bloodline rites, votive offerings, and sacred regulation of reef danger.
districts.visualIdentity: Shrine / Sacred Place Civic Hall, Roadside Shrine, Shrine Sacred Place Lantern Monument, Votive Grove Nook, wet paths, shell markers, black stone, and red reef symbols.

districts.name: Harbor Campus
districts.civicPurpose: Harbor rites, arrival, departure, public vows, mourning, and sacred-facing civic identity.
districts.visualIdentity: S5 Harbor Campus, Harbor Basilica, S5 Harbor Arch, S5 Harbor Bridgelet, water-facing plazas, basilica windows, wet stairs, and banners.

wonder.name: Sharkjaw Tide Citadel of the Red Reef
wonder.symbolicRole: Predatory force disciplined into tide law and reef protection.
wonder.visualRole: Far wonder-scale sharkjaw citadel of black stone, red reef accents, shell inlay, water-facing towers, and predatory silhouettes made official.
wonder.civicRole: Ultimate civic emblem of Bloodreef's protective menace and maritime authority.
storyHooks: a public crisis tests whether sharkjaw law protects or devours
the Red Reef symbol becomes a promise rather than a threat

beacon.name: Nursery Hall Beacon
beacon.symbolicRole: Warning rooted in protection of young.
beacon.visualRole: Blue-lit beacon rising from nursery civic space with shell inlay, guarded water approaches, nursery pillars, and protected thresholds.
beacon.civicRole: Public signal heart for emergency coordination and nursery legitimacy.
storyHooks: a Beacon warning turns every dangerous citizen toward protection
someone tries to use nursery panic as political cover

seatOfPower.name: Shell Spiral Windows Tide-Seal Citadel
seatOfPower.symbolicRole: Reef authority made lawful, cyclical, and watchful.
seatOfPower.visualRole: Central tide-seal citadel with shell spiral windows, black stone, blue lamps, reef arches, archive courts, and water-facing authority routes.
seatOfPower.civicRole: Government center for tide law, nursery protection, cargo legitimacy, public works permits, shrine rights, and harbor ceremony.
storyHooks: a tide-seal record changes who may enter a protected basin
the Citadel must choose between trade speed and nursery safety

majorLandmarks.name: Cargo Yard
majorLandmarks.symbolicRole: Public trust in goods under dangerous guardianship.
majorLandmarks.visualRole: Crates, ropes, inventory hall, gold-seal monument, seal counters, dock workers, and black reef walls.
majorLandmarks.civicRole: Manages goods entering and leaving the city and anchors maritime reputation.
storyHooks: a missing cargo seal changes public trust

majorLandmarks.name: Survey Hall
majorLandmarks.symbolicRole: The dangerous reef measured, repaired, and made safe.
majorLandmarks.visualRole: Maps, tide-gate diagrams, permit boards, blue lamps, measuring instruments, and wet public-works plazas.
majorLandmarks.civicRole: Controls reef repair, dock permissions, harbor route safety, and tide-gate work.
storyHooks: a permit delay redirects a crowd
a repair map reveals a hidden route

majorLandmarks.name: Roadside Shrine
majorLandmarks.symbolicRole: Reef danger remembered, mourned, and ritually bounded.
majorLandmarks.visualRole: Black-stone shrine path with votive lanterns, red reef symbols, shell markers, and Votive Grove Nook.
majorLandmarks.civicRole: Hosts offerings, vows, mourning, bloodline memory, and sacred regulation of dangerous water.
storyHooks: a votive record names someone official history omits
a shrine path becomes the safest route during panic

majorLandmarks.name: Harbor Basilica
majorLandmarks.symbolicRole: Arrival, departure, mourning, and public vow at the water threshold.
majorLandmarks.visualRole: Water-facing basilica with Harbor Arch, Harbor Bridgelet, wet stairs, windows, banners, and ceremonial crowds.
majorLandmarks.civicRole: Hosts ritual arrivals, departures, public vows, maritime mourning, and sacred harbor ceremonies.
storyHooks: a vow made under the Harbor Arch becomes binding
a departure ceremony exposes social rank

districtDioramaGuidance: S1 must show S1 Nursery Hall, Nursery Hall Beacon, S1 Nursery Pillar, S1 Nursery Kiosk, shell inlay, protected basins, sharkfolk guardians, and controlled thresholds.
S2 must show Warehousing / Logistics Civic Hall, Cargo Yard, Warehousing Logistics Gold-Seal Monument, Inventory Hall Nook, crates, ropes, dock workers, and ledger boards.
S3 must show Public Works Authority Hall, Survey Hall, Blue-Lamp Monument, Permit Yard Nook, tide-gate diagrams, wet stone plazas, and survey crews.
S4 must show Shrine / Sacred Place Civic Hall, Roadside Shrine, Shrine Sacred Place Lantern Monument, Votive Grove Nook, shrine lanterns, offering stones, and reef paths.
S5 must show S5 Harbor Campus, Harbor Basilica, S5 Harbor Arch, S5 Harbor Bridgelet, water-facing stairs, basilica windows, and ritual crowds.
mapBackgroundGuidance: Use a dark reef-harbor capital with black basalt, red reef accents, shell spiral windows, blue civic lamps, nursery basins, cargo yards, survey halls, shrines, harbor basilica, and canal bridges over water.

The Sharkjaw Tide Citadel of the Red Reef should be the wonder-scale anchor; Shell Spiral Windows Tide-Seal Citadel should be central authority; Nursery Hall Beacon should visually tie danger to protection.
Keep the city intimidating but civilized: nursery protection, logistics, public works, shrines, and harbor ceremony must be visible.
requiredVisualElements: Sharkjaw Tide Citadel of the Red Reef
black basalt and wet reef stone
elementsToAvoid: lawless dock grime as dominant identity
